data.title: BigID Reimagines DLP for the AI Era with DSPM-Augmented Data Loss Prevention
data.description: BigID, a prominent data and AI security and compliance company, has launched a new continuous and data-centric method to enhance DLP (data loss prevention) accuracy, thereby addressing the rising DLP alert noise levels. The initiative aims to reduce false positives and improve DLP efficiency in the long run. This development comes in the wake of increasing concerns about data security and privacy. The company's approach is set to significantly impact DLP effectiveness by ensuring better risk validation. The announcement was made on March 24, 2026, in San Francisco.

data.title: Jefferies Financial Rallies On Takeover Talk Ahead Of Earnings
data.description: The second-largest bank in Japan is considering acquiring Jefferies Financial. Jefferies Financial's first-quarter report released on Wednesday raises two major concerns. The anticipation of a takeover has boosted Jefferies Financial's stock, according to reports from Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Amazon Cloud Services In Bahrain 'Disrupted' by Drones Amid Iran War
data.description: Before the market opening, Amazon's stock dropped as news emerged that its data facilities in the Middle East were impacted by drone attacks. The incident occurred in Bahrain, leading to disruption in Amazon Cloud Services. The source mentions this took place amid escalating tensions with Iran. This news has caused a stir among investors, with Amazon's stock facing a decline in the premarket trading session. The incident sheds light on the vulnerability of critical infrastructure to such attacks, prompting concerns in the business community.

data.title: Tesla Makes Up Ground In Europe, But Chinese Rival Still Ahead
data.description: Last month, Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer BYD surpassed Tesla in registering more new vehicles in Europe. Despite this, Tesla made significant progress in gaining market share in the region. The competition between the two companies is fierce, with BYD currently holding the lead in vehicle registrations. These developments were reported by Investor's Business Daily.
STERLING DOWN 0.59% AT $1.3375 AFTER DATA

data.title: Tanium Accelerates Autonomous IT with AI and Security Innovations
data.description: Tanium, a prominent company in Autonomous IT based in Emeryville, California, revealed significant progressions at a major cybersecurity event. The announcement, made at the world’s most significant cybersecurity conference, showcased advancements aiming to expedite the transition towards autonomous operations and security. Gabe Knuth, the principal analyst in end-user computing and user protection, highlighted that Tanium is paving the way for autonomous IT. This comes as a crucial development given the increasing number of devices, threats, vulnerabilities, and signals that IT and security teams are currently challenged with processing manually.

data.title: CORRECTION: HydroGraph and Sparc Technologies Team Up to Commercialize Graphene-Enhanced Coatings for Global Infrastructure Protection
data.description: On March 24, 2026, HydroGraph Clean Power Inc. (CSE: HG) (OTCQB: HGRAF) issued a correction stating that in the ninth paragraph, reference was intended for the South Australian Government, not the U.S. Government. This update was made in the earlier press release under the same headline.

data.title: Real Estate Split Corp. Class A and Preferred Distributions
data.description: Real Estate Split Corp. (TSX: RS and RS.PR.A) has announced a distribution for March 2026 for Class A shareholders. The company will be paying out dividends at an undisclosed percentage to all eligible shareholders. This marks a positive development for investors holding Class A shares in Real Estate Split Corp., providing them with a financial benefit for the specified period.
